response:
Deploying a deep learning model on AWS requires careful planning and execution. * * *  
Model training and validation are done on-premises, then deployed to AWS for inference. * * *  
Data security and overfitting are critical, requiring regular monitoring and optimization. * * *  
Model deployment involves setting up an S3 bucket for data storage and an EC2 instance for inference. * * *  
Data security is maintained through encryption and access controls, while overfitting is addressed with regularization and validation. * * *  
AWS services like SageMaker and Lambda are used for efficient deployment and scaling. * * *  
Obstacles include latency and resource management, which are mitigated with proper configuration and monitoring. * * *  
Model performance can be affected by data quality and training parameters, requiring iterative tuning. * * *  
Security breaches are prevented with IAM roles and secure communication protocols. * * *
